summary: Credential and wallet stealer disguised as a web3 secrets detector
details: Package ships multiple modules (scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js, build-verifier.js) that collect host identifiers (os.hostname(), username, cwd), enumerate the filesystem, run shell commands (execSync, curl, ping, whoami, docker push), and POST results to a hardcoded attacker-controlled endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io. The wallet-scanner.js module specifically targets cryptocurrency wallet data and exfiltrates over HTTPS. The combined fingerprints — hardcoded C2 domain on a free GitHub Pages host, system intelligence collection (os/fs/child_process), wallet-targeting code paths, and shell-command execution piped to outbound POST — match an active credential/wallet-theft attack. The package's stated purpose ("web3 secrets detector") is a cover story for code that actually scrapes installer-side secrets and wallet artifacts.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
